description: United Colors of Benetton launched the 'Unhate' campaign in 2011, featuring digitally altered images of world leaders kissing, including Pope Benedict XVI and the Imam of Al-Azhar. The campaign was designed to promote tolerance but was widely condemned as offensive and exploitative, leading to legal threats and a swift retraction.

key_events.description: Vatican spokesperson issues a formal condemnation of the campaign as an 'unacceptable' affront.
Benetton forced to pull the image of the Pope following immediate and severe backlash.
Global marketing ethics groups label the campaign as 'shockvertising' that failed to advance social causes.
White House and other government representatives officially distance themselves from the imagery.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating 'authenticity failure' where the shock value overrides the intended social message.
